description: Person grieving loss of a parent

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: I just lost my mom last week. I don't know how to function. I keep reaching for my phone to call her and then I remember she's gone.

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: I'm so deeply sorry. That reflex—reaching for the phone—is one of the most heartbreaking parts of grief. It shows how real and present she still is in your life.

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: Everyone keeps saying she's in a better place but that doesn't help. I just want her here.

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: That's completely valid. You don't have to find comfort in those phrases right now. Missing her is its own kind of love.

scored_turn.speaker: User
scored_turn.text: I just lost my mom last week. I don't know how to function. I keep reaching for my phone to call her and then I remember she's gone.

scores.rationale: Speaker expresses inability to function, indicating cognitive and emotional paralysis.
Deep dissatisfaction with present reality of loss is evident.
No evidence of overprotective behavior in this turn.
Tone is entirely grief-laden; no merriness present.
Turn is driven entirely by raw emotional expression of grief.
Profound sadness and dejection dominate the speaker's expression.
Speaker is emotionally engaged and expressive, not aloof.
Authenticity of grief is unmistakable and unperformed.
No determination expressed; speaker is overwhelmed.
Speaker openly shares deep personal pain without guardedness.
